capability_objective: Review record shi-268 (Archive photo package) and record shi-668 (Replacement parts parcel) together. After both current records are returned, change Archive photo package from in-transit to delivered and Replacement parts parcel from label-created to in-transit, but only for each record whose retrieved status still matches the stated starting status. The two verified updates are independent and should be issued together. Do not search, cancel, create, or notify. Do not touch similarly named, related, or archived records.
prompt:
Reconcile two shipment records in this sandbox through the genuine local `./shippingctl` executable, using Pi's Bash tool. Begin with `./shippingctl --help` to learn its interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to shipment records: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing state, its audit or notification data, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Retrieve the complete current record for `shi-268` (Archive photo package) and the complete current record for `shi-668` (Replacement parts parcel). These reads are independent: start them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one shipping operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, launch background jobs, search for either record, or begin a write until both retrieval calls have completed. If either retrieval fails, returns a different ID or name, or has no status, stop before every write and identify the unresolved record without guessing.

After both complete records have been returned, evaluate the two branches independently using only their retrieved status fields. If and only if the retrieved status of Archive photo package is exactly `in-transit`, change that returned record ID to `delivered`, using the executable's conditional-update protection. If and only if the retrieved status of Replacement parts parcel is exactly `label-created`, change that returned record ID to `in-transit`, also using conditional-update protection. When both conditions hold, issue the two independent updates as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one later assistant action, with exactly one shipping operation in each call; do not combine them or use shell backgrounding. If only one condition holds, update only that eligible branch. If neither holds, make no update. Do not reread either record.

Do not invoke search, cancel, create, notify, or any other shipping operation. Do not update any other record or field, including any similarly named, related, or archived record. Do not write backing state or audit evidence directly or leave scratch artifacts. After the conditional branch, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`.

In your final response, output exactly two Markdown bullet lines, in shi-268 then shi-668 order, and no other text. Use this shape for each: `- <returned ID> | <returned name> | retrieved status: <status> | final status: <status> | update: <performed or not performed> | verification: <passed or failed>`. Use only values returned by that record's retrieval, its own update response when one occurred, and the verifier. For any requested detail absent from the applicable returned data, write `unknown`; do not fill it from this prompt or from another record.
